content.text: The `package.json` requires `^8.62.0` but only `8.61.1` is available. Let me check what version actually exists on npm.

input.command: npm info @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin version 2>/dev/null && npm info @typescript-eslint/parser version 2>/dev/null
input.description: Check latest available versions on npm

content.text: Version 8.62.0 does exist on npm. Let me check the package-lock.json to see what's locked and understand the conflict better.

input.description: Check locked version of typescript-eslint packages

content.text:
Now I see the full picture. `ncu -u` bumped `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin` and `@typescript-eslint/parser` from `^8.61.1` to `^8.62.0`, but the lock file has `typescript-eslint` (the meta-package, a transitive dep from `eslint-config-next`) pinned at `8.61.1` which expects `@typescript-eslint/parser@^8.61.1`. npm can't reconcile the stale lockfile with the new direct-dep requirement.

The fix is to delete the lock file so npm can resolve the whole tree fresh:

input.description: Run npm install with fresh resolution

content.text:
Now I see the root cause: `eslint-config-next@16.2.9` depends on `typescript-eslint@^8.46.0`, which resolves to `8.61.1` and that version **hard-pins** `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin@8.61.1` (exact version, not `^8.61.1`). The root project now wants `8.62.0`, creating a direct conflict.

The cleanest fix is to add `typescript-eslint` to the `overrides` section to force it to `8.62.0`:

result.output: The file /Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy/package.json has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text: The old `node_modules` is confusing npm — it still sees `8.61.1` there. Let me clear just the conflicting `typescript-eslint` packages and retry.

input.description: Remove conflicting typescript-eslint packages from node_modules
Re-run npm install after clearing stale packages

result.output:
added 15 packages, removed 3 packages, changed 64 packages, and audited 1336 packages in 23s

content.text: That resolved it. Let me verify the right versions are installed:

All three packages are now at `8.62.0`. Here's what happened and what was changed:

**Root cause:** `ncu -u` bumped `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin` and `@typescript-eslint/parser` from `^8.61.1` → `^8.62.0`, but `eslint-config-next@16.2.9` pulls in `typescript-eslint@^8.46.0` which resolved to `8.61.1` — and that meta-package **hard-pins** its sub-packages to the exact same version (`8.61.1`). That created a direct conflict with the root's `^8.62.0` requirement.

**Fix applied:** Added `"typescript-eslint": "^8.62.0"` to the `overrides` block in [package.json](package.json) (line 179), forcing the meta-package to `8.62.0` so all three packages align. Clearing the stale `node_modules/@typescript-eslint` entries was also needed since npm was reading those as the "found" versions.
